articles.summary: The results are in: Amplitude is a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Digital Analytics Solutions, Q3 2025 [4], receiving the highest “Current Offering” category score of all evaluated digital analytics vendors.

articles.title: 👀 See why Amplitude earned 5/5 scores in 21 criteria.

articles.title: FROM SEARCH TO SOCIAL: INSIDE THE BILLIONS SHIFTING TO SOCIAL-FIRST MARKETING
articles.summary: Unilever's decision to shift half its media budget to social reflects a broader trend, with 80% of marketers reallocating funds from traditional channels. Social media has become the core of discovery, engagement, and purchasing, with ad spend projected to hit $406 billion globally by 2029. The 2025 Impact of Social Media Marketing Report found that 87% of leaders plan to increase paid social spending, and over 80% expect to increase influencer and organic social investment. Brands like Wild, Duolingo, Lowe's, and Marks & Spencer show how social-first strategies can drive measurable sales growth, especially with Gen Z and Millennial audiences.

articles.title: MOST DEVS BELIEVE AI IS GOING TO KILL 'MOST OR ALL' OF THEIR COMPANY'S MARKETING DEPARTMENT
articles.summary: Developers overwhelmingly see AI as capable of replacing large parts of marketing, with nearly 75% saying it could do most or all of the work. In contrast, far fewer marketers believe they could replicate developers' jobs, highlighting a confidence gap between the two fields. The findings reflect developers' deep integration of AI in their daily work, where 90% already depend on it for efficiency and problem-solving.

articles.title: SHOPPERS SEEK FEATURES, NOT OUTCOMES, ON LLM
articles.summary: LLMs are changing homepage writing by shifting focus to specific features over broad outcomes and reducing the influence of brand recognition for smaller buyers. Users search for precise functionality, so products must clearly list features for LLMs to recommend them. While large enterprises still favor well-known brands, smaller businesses and consumers increasingly choose products that match their exact needs, even from lesser-known companies.

articles.title: HOW BRANDS CAN WIN BIG WITH INCLUSION STRATEGY
articles.summary: Inclusive branding is proving to be a commercial growth driver, with one study showing short-term sales gains of 3.5%, long-term growth of 16.5%, and loyalty increases of 15% when brands adopt inclusive advertising. Mattel's Barbie is a strong example, seeing a 63% revenue jump between 2015 and 2022 after shifting toward inclusivity, while Bud Light serves as a cautionary example for alienating a core base by misjudging identity tensions. To succeed, brands should conduct an identity audit of their customers and brand DNA, run incremental tests to refine messaging and stakeholder alignment, and fully integrate inclusivity into governance, partnerships, and company culture.

articles.title: AI SEARCH RANK TRACKERS ARE LYING TO YOU
articles.summary: Most AI search visibility tools overpromise because they rely on synthetic prompts and lack access to real user data. Reliable tracking is difficult due to query variance, personalization, model differences, and changing outputs, making accurate measurement of AI-driven search visibility nearly impossible. AI is still in its early stages, so it's important to understand each tool's data sources and limitations before trusting their reports.

articles.title: LINKEDIN ADDS MORE POST-LEVEL ANALYTICS UPDATES
articles.summary: LinkedIn users will now get notifications at key intervals, such as 3 and 7 days after posting, showing how their content drives impressions, profile views, and follower growth. The updates, delivered directly in notifications, build on LinkedIn's May analytics refresh and support its broader push to attract creators with better insights and monetization options. Users can manage notification frequency to avoid overload.

articles.title: AMAZON'S EXIT FROM GOOGLE ADS TRIGGERED A TRAFFIC SURGE — BUT ROI DIDN'T FOLLOW
articles.summary: Amazon's sudden exit from Google Shopping ads in July caused immediate disruption across ad auctions, leading to more clicks and impressions but weaker returns for most advertisers. Overall clicks rose 7.8% and CPCs fell 8.3%, but conversion value declined 5.5% and ROAS dropped 4.4%, showing that extra traffic often failed to translate into profitable sales. Electronics was the only major category to see true gains, with conversions up 81.3% and ROAS up 7.1%, as brands like Best Buy and Apple could match Amazon's speed and pricing. Most other sectors, including Apparel & Accessories and Sporting Goods, fell into the “volume trap,” gaining traffic but losing efficiency as Amazon-seeking shoppers expected unmatched price and convenience. Advertisers must track value, not just volume, and focus on sustainable advantages rather than chasing displaced Amazon traffic.

articles.title: SCALING CONTENT AT DROPBOX WITH AI AND TASTE
articles.summary: The Dropbox content team replaced the traditional waterfall method of large sequential campaigns with a more agile structure that allows faster pivots and continuous learning. The team was organized into four pods that focus on quick-turn experiments, explanatory content, customer stories, and thought leadership, all supported by real-time data and reporting. By connecting an automated content calendar to workflows and KPIs, the group tripled production for Dropbox Sign without increasing budget, while agency partners and AI tools provided overflow capacity to prevent burnout and maintain quality.

articles.title: STUPIDEST MARKETING MOVE + HOW TO WIN ON SOCIAL WITH GARY VEE (34 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: Gary Vaynerchuk recommends identifying content that performs well organically, tweaking it slightly, and amplifying it to drive sales or registrations. He advises giving value first through the “jab, jab, jab, right hook” approach, where giving builds brand and credibility before asking. Interest should focus on niche passions over follower count, guided by the PAC framework of platforms, algorithms, and culture. Spotting micro-trends that can evolve into long-term cultural movements and avoiding last-touch attribution helps preserve brand impact.

articles.title: WHY NIKE CAN GET AWAY WITH ‘JUST DO IT' AND YOUR B2B BRAND CAN'T
articles.summary: B2C messaging is often focused on giving intangible meaning to products, such as identity or inspiration, while B2B messaging needs to demonstrate tangible value with clarity.

articles.title: DISTRIBUTION > CONTENT
articles.summary: Every campaign must start with how it reaches and moves the right audience, or it fails.

articles.title: USE AI FOR YOUR MARKETING INTERVIEW “PROJECT”
articles.summary: Tips on saving 20-30 hours of unpaid labor for your next interview project.
